refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall minimize cycle time by optimizing load, travel, and dump phases to meet the customer-defined cycle time target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad; decomposing it into sub-FRs for each phase of the cycle (load, travel, dump) allows targeted design parameters and clearer verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure cycle time p95 under representative duty cycles and site conditions, verifying each sub-phase meets its allocated time budget.

candidate_dps: Automated load operation with optimized bucket control
Optimized travel path planning and vehicle speed control
Automated dump operation with precise positioning
selected_dp.selected_dp: Automated load operation with optimized bucket control

potential_sources: Load phase automation depends on vehicle control and teleoperation coordination
Travel and dump phases have interfaces but are logically separable
coupling_check.notes: DPs for load, travel, and dump phases are largely decoupled with well-defined interfaces, enabling modular design.

assumptions_risks: Assumes accurate sensor data and reliable teleoperation network link
Risk of cycle time degradation if any phase underperforms
High safety criticality requires robust fail-safe mechanisms
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicit DP for coordination and safety monitoring across phases to mitigate integration risks.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical and control systems responsible for load phase execution including bucket actuation and sensors

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Send load phase status and sensor data to NGHE Solution and Safety Services

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receive load operation commands from Teleoperation and NGHE Solution

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Send safety alerts related to load phase to Safety Services

module_containment_scores.notes: High diffusion due to coordination role; limited containment of load phase physical control
Strong containment of load phase DP; low diffusion as physical actuation is localized
Moderate containment for remote commands; some diffusion due to shared control
Low containment; mainly receives alerts and monitors safety

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Load phase DP is best contained within the Vehicle module to minimize diffusion and ensure tight control over physical actuation.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Teleoperation and NGHE Solution modules
Risk of delayed safety alert propagation if interfaces are not robust
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts and redundancy for safety alerts and tasking commands to mitigate interface risks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: FR refinement is clear and sub-FRs are logically decomposed; selected DP aligns well with load phase FR.

diffusion_risks: Coordination across modules may cause diffusion if interfaces are not well managed
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module shows strong containment for load phase DP, minimizing diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data and cycle time measurements at Vehicle module boundaries.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of load phase DP to Vehicle IM; interfaces to other modules are well defined.

recommended_revisions: Add explicit DP and IM for coordination and safety monitoring across phases.
Strengthen interface definitions to reduce diffusion risks.

decision.justification: While the load phase DP and IM assignment are sound, the overall cycle time FR requires explicit coordination and safety monitoring DPs and IMs to address integration and safety risks.
next_level_sub_frs: Coordinate cycle phases to optimize overall cycle time
Ensure safety compliance during cycle execution

revise.why: To include coordination and safety monitoring DPs that address integration and high safety criticality.

assumptions: Accurate sensor data for load phase
Safety alerts are timely and effective
open_risks: Potential delays in safety alert propagation
Cycle time degradation if any phase underperforms
evidence_needed: Telemetry data demonstrating load phase cycle time performance
